diagrams.title: Positive Definite Matrix Transformation
diagrams.description: Shows how a positive definite matrix transforms a unit circle into an ellipse, demonstrating the geometric interpretation where all eigenvalues are positive and the quadratic form x^T Ax > 0 for all non-zero vectors x.
diagrams.purpose: Visualizes the geometric meaning of positive definiteness - the transformation stretches but never flips the unit circle, indicating all principal axes point in the same general direction

diagrams.title: Simple Two-State Markov Chain
diagrams.description: A basic Markov chain transition diagram showing two states (A and B) with transition probabilities. The transition matrix is symmetric and positive definite, illustrating the connection between Markov chains and positive definite matrices.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how Markov chain transition matrices work and introduces the concept of stochastic matrices that can be positive definite

diagrams.title: Eigenvalue Distribution Comparison
diagrams.description: A number line visualization comparing eigenvalues of positive definite matrices (all positive) versus indefinite matrices (mixed positive and negative), with a 2x2 matrix example showing how to compute eigenvalues.
elements: 2x2 matrix example with eigenvalues λ1=3, λ2=1
Labels showing positive definite vs indefinite
diagrams.purpose: Clarifies the eigenvalue criterion for positive definiteness - all eigenvalues must be positive - which is essential for understanding Markov chain convergence properties
